mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate on future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2022 FEDFUNDS vintage at 2.33 with clear hiking trajectory
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent during the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation and a rising federal funds rate force real discount rates higher and squeeze corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a 20-quarter rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a contractionary shock signals recovering manufacturing output and inventory restocking, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases after 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not positive in more than 50 percent of instances following a rebound in INDPRO from two consecutive declines.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in real retail and food services sales signals weakening consumer demand that translates into lower expected revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of months when RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real gross domestic product growth signals broad-based expansion in economic activity that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and increase in nonfarm payrolls signals an overheating labor market that amplifies wage-cost pressures and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from declining UNRATE and rising PAYEMS does not produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index forces upward revisions to terminal rate expectations and compresses equity valuations through higher real discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.5 percent month-over-month.
